extraction.one_line_summary: ALAPHILIPPE Julian won the Stage 4 ITT with steep 3km finishing climb, taking race lead by 1:31 over CONTADOR Alberto.
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step's echelon management on stages 1-3 protected Alaphilippe while isolating Contador, creating cumulative fatigue before ITT.
Alaphilippe's uphill TT superiority over pure stage race specialists signals vulnerability in traditional climber time trial preparation.

rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: dominated uphill ITT against specialist stage racers, suggesting week-long GC capability beyond punchy one-day racing
rider_signals.CONTADOR Alberto: negative: caught behind echelons stage 1, chasing through stages 2-3 created pre-ITT fatigue that compromised his climbing TT strength
rider_signals.DEMARE Arnaud: negative: lost yellow on uphill ITT finish, confirming sprinters cannot defend GC once steep gradients enter route profile
